title: [InstCombine] Fold (trunc X to i1) & !iszero(X & Pow2)) -> (X & (1 | Pow2)) == (1 | Pow2)

tests.source_program:
define i1 @i64_trunc_and_icmp_high_bit(i64 %x) {
  %trunc = trunc i64 %x to i1
  %and = and i64 %x, 2048
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %and, 0
  %ret = and i1 %icmp, %trunc
  ret i1 %ret
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary examples all use i8 type. This test uses i64 with a higher power-of-two bit (2048 = 2^11), which is outside the documented test coverage.

alive2.src:
define i1 @i64_trunc_and_icmp_high_bit(i64 %x) {
  %trunc = trunc i64 %x to i1
  %and = and i64 %x, 2048
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %and, 0
  %ret = and i1 %icmp, %trunc
  ret i1 %ret
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @i64_trunc_and_icmp_high_bit(i64 %x) {
  %1 = and i64 %x, 2049
  %ret = icmp eq i64 %1, 2049
  ret i1 %ret
}

----------------------------------------

#0:
  %trunc = trunc i64 %x to i1
  %and = and i64 %x, 2048
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %and, 0
  %ret = and i1 %icmp, %trunc
  ret i1 %ret
}
=>
define i1 @i64_trunc_and_icmp_high_bit(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = and i64 %x, 2049
  %ret = icmp eq i64 %#1, 2049
  ret i1 %ret
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i1 @logical_or_not_trunc_eq_pow2_select(i16 %x, i16 %c1) {
  %t = shl i16 1, %c1
  %t1 = and i16 %t, %x
  %icmp = icmp eq i16 %t1, 0
  %trunc = trunc i16 %x to i1
  %not = xor i1 %trunc, true
  %ret = select i1 %not, i1 true, i1 %icmp
  ret i1 %ret
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @logical_or_not_trunc_eq_pow2_select(i16 %x, i16 %c1) {
  %t = shl i16 1, %c1
  %freeze = freeze i16 %t
  %mask = or i16 %freeze, 1
  %and = and i16 %x, %mask
  %icmp = icmp ne i16 %and, %mask
  ret i1 %icmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Uses i16 type with variable shift for power-of-two (not a constant), and uses select for logical OR pattern. The summary focuses on constant power-of-two values and doesn't cover variable shifts.

alive2.src:
define i1 @logical_or_not_trunc_eq_pow2_select(i16 %x, i16 %c1) {
  %t = shl i16 1, %c1
  %t1 = and i16 %t, %x
  %icmp = icmp eq i16 %t1, 0
  %trunc = trunc i16 %x to i1
  %not = xor i1 %trunc, true
  %ret = select i1 %not, i1 true, i1 %icmp
  ret i1 %ret
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @logical_or_not_trunc_eq_pow2_select(i16 %x, i16 %c1) {
  %t = shl nuw i16 1, %c1
  %1 = freeze i16 %t
  %2 = or i16 %1, 1
  %3 = and i16 %x, %2
  %ret = icmp ne i16 %3, %2
  ret i1 %ret
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @i64_trunc_and_icmp_pow2(i64 %x) {
  %trunc = trunc i64 %x to i1
  %and = and i64 %x, 16
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %and, 0
  %ret = and i1 %trunc, %icmp
  ret i1 %ret
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: 64-bit integer type (i64) is not tested in any existing cases. All known tests use i8 or i32 types only, missing coverage for wider integer widths.

alive2.src:

  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %and, 0
  %ret = and i1 %trunc, %icmp
  ret i1 %ret
}
